# identity-service

> QARoom identity-service: users, communities-as-tenants, memberships, sessions, and JWT/JWKS. Milestone 2.

The machine-readable contract is `openapi.yaml` (OpenAPI 3.0, generated from Zod).
Live capabilities are served at `GET /system/capabilities` in MCP-tool-shaped form,
and current observable state (including signing-key status) at `GET /system/state`.
Tokens are ES256 with a `kid` header; verify against `GET /jwks.json` (root path).
Every error is RFC 7807 `application/problem+json` with `retryable`, `next_actions`,
and `failure_domain`. A request naming a non-existent community fails in the
`tenant_resolution` domain — identity is the community registry.

## Operations
- POST /api/users                                  — createUser (mutating; Idempotency-Key required)
- GET  /api/users/{userId}                         — getUser
- POST /api/communities                            — createCommunity (mutating; Idempotency-Key required)
- POST /api/communities/{communityId}/members      — addMembership (mutating; Idempotency-Key required)
- GET  /api/communities/{communityId}/members      — listMembers
- POST /api/sessions                               — createSession (mutating; issues an access token)
- GET  /jwks.json                                  — getJwks (public verification keys)
- GET  /system/state                               — getSystemState
- GET  /system/capabilities                        — getSystemCapabilities

## Docs
- Contract: ./openapi.yaml
- Agent reference: ./AGENTS.md
- Repo conventions: /AGENTS.md, /docs
